column9: Vinyl Driven Twisted Disco
column10:
Ad Fontes (Latin - “to the source”) is a mini sound camp focused on vinyl based DJs across of the spectrum of the electronic music frontier. We are an open camp and have begun the process of lining up DJs. 

Overall Ad Fontes is a small camp (8+ members) and needs vehicle access to set/ break camp. The first members will arrive Wednesday to set camp. We are open as to placement and would enjoy a level (ish) spot with consideration to minimize sound bleed from the larger camps. Our sound system is modest (~ 5000W). If it’s possible to look out over an olen space that would be awesome. 

We will break camp Sunday, late morning. 

We need about 4000 square feet, with roughly 30 feet of frontage, but we’re flexible. 

We have all the necessary gear and experience to adhere to the guidelines for this event. As the camp host, this is my second To the Moon and I am familiar with event planning and remote camping.

column9:
Patchwork Petal Parlor:

Wander into camp through the colorful winged pathways of The Lady Garden. Come inside the Patchwork Parlor and sit a spell in our cozy lounge. Visit our origami station, a partner piece to our art installation "Out of the Chrysallis", to find a peaceful moment of creative Regenesis.
column10: Our camp features three main public offerings. We will have a solar garden with paver stone pathways flocking either side of the entrance to our lounge area. This will be filled with solar powered butterflies, flowers and all sorts of garden delights. Inside our highly decorated lounge you will find a variety of playatech and seating options centering, conversation pit style, around a pillow filled cuddle puddle. We hope to also provide some fans for a nice refreshing breeze. Just off the lounge, will also be an origami station. This is the partner piece to our art installation "Out of the Chrysallis". It will feature a craft table, chairs and origami paper with instructions for making butterflies.

column9:
✨Reconnect is your place to relax, refresh, and recharge.
A space to regulate, get grounded, take a seat, and hydrate. We’re always open, and you’re always welcome.

Nestled close to the energy of our beautiful sound camps, Reconnect offers calming vibes without pulling you away from the action. Cool down, catch your breath, refill your canteen at our water station, and settle into our ample seating before heading back out to enjoy the experience.

We’re here to help you reset so you can keep going strong.

Come by, take a moment, and reconnect.
column10:
✨ Reconnect Camp is a community stabilization unit offering participants a place to lean into radical self-reliance and regain their footing. We create a calming, supportive environment where anyone can pause, regulate, and regroup. With ample seating and plenty of water, Reconnect is designed to help you stabilize before stepping back into the experience.
We are proud to partner with Sanctuary, hosting harm‑reduction materials and information to support the well-being of our community. Reconnect is also on the radar of Rangers, helping ensure that anyone who may need Sanctuary is noticed and supported. Our team members will be completing Ranger training so they’re informed, prepared, and aligned with event protocols. Our crew includes someone who has worked with Sanctuary at past TTMs, a participant who is a therapist, and another with a background in holistic management—each bringing valuable insight to our mission.
Reconnect is embracing the principle of Civic Responsibility, showing up for our community with intention, care, and accountability.
🌞 Daytime Offerings (Pending Art Grant Funding)
We hope to host a DIY art space, because creativity can be deeply grounding. Our project, “Art of the Heart,” will invite participants to slow down, express themselves, and reconnect through hands-on making.
🌿 Sacred Smoke Workshop (Pending Art Grant)
We also plan to offer a herb-bundle crafting station “Sacred Smoke Workshop” a grounding, sensory experience available throughout the day and night. This space will give participants another way to center themselves and reconnect with intention.

column9: Wet your whistle at the Surreal Saloon! Saspirilla, face, body & nail support to get you READY for the burn & to reset/recharge afterwards. Relax and get WEIRD with us! Self-care has never been so surreal.
column10:
Last year was our first year *formally* bringing the Surreal Saloon To The Moon (though we've brought it informally the year before!). We offered a pretty extensive self-serve nail bar & art experience. Folks had a lot of fun and would often wander from the thrift store / clothing camp next door over to us to complete the look! On Sunday we hosted had a pretty lovely recovery offering.

A misspelling on our sign last year transformed the "Salon" into a "Saloon" and made us all giggle. SO this year, we're playing with that twist of fate! Western themes & saspirilla. We've applied for an art grant to bring this vision to *the next level*, but regardless, we'll be bringing good clean surreal fun to our fellow burners!

column9: "Is it fun?" If it is, we do it. If it's not, we cover it in liquor and do it anyway.
column10:
Just People has been around a few years. Our theme? We do what we like.

If you want to reduce us to labels, we are a bar, a DJ booth, a dancefloor, a pop-up entertainment show, but we're not just labels, we're Just People. Tired of boots and cats? What about booze and hats. Don't be bored at a bar, come draw at a bar. We are where the only unce unce unce comes from a German Sparkle Party, and where you might get offered a Ham Slam if you ask the right questions.

We're a reptile house. We're a star-spangled rodeo. Sometimes we're a 4-minute track of cats meowing. We're the place you start your morning with a mimosa and end your night with an expanded vocabulary. We as a Camp ask the question, "Is it fun?" If it is, we do it. If it's not, we cover it in liquor and do it anyway.

column9:
Step out of the chaos and into the glow.
Black Lit Moon is a sanctuary for the senses, a late-night lounge where ultraviolet light transforms everything into something a little more magical. Dripping in neon art, soft textures, and slow energy, this camp is built for decompression, connection, and quiet awe. 
Sink into soft seating, let the black lights paint your skin in color, and breathe. Whether you're coming down from the night, escaping the heat, or just craving a softer frequency, this is your place to land.
Expect ambient soundscapes, glowing installations, and a rotating canvas of bodies and art illuminated in electric hues. No pressure. No performance. Just presence.
Find the moon. Follow the glow. Stay as long as you need.

column9: Camp Whimsy Van Winkle is a playful, immersive pop-up and lounge space offering a rotating collection of funky fashion, accessories, and adornments within a gifting-based experience. Come explore, get styled, and recharge in a cozy, whimsical environment.
column10:
Camp Whimsy Van Winkle will be a gifting-based pop-up and lounge space featuring clothing racks, tables, and a shaded seating area for rest and connection. The setup will function as both an interactive styling experience and a soft landing space where participants can recharge.

The pop-up will include curated racks of clothing and accessories, along with mirrors and areas for people to explore self-expression through adornment. The lounge space will have rugs, seating, and ambient decor to create a welcoming, whimsical environment.

I may host an informal, community-driven fashion moment or runway-style gathering, potentially at a shared space like the Grotto. This would be low-pressure and participatory, inviting attendees to style themselves using pieces from the camp or their own looks and celebrate radical self-expression together. No formal production or infrastructure beyond the camp setup is planned at this time.

column9:
Abiding Grove – Dudeism Camp

Step into a laid-back sanctuary of play, story, and connection. Abiding Grove channels the spirit of Dudeism with a welcoming lounge atmosphere where burners can gather, unwind, and engage.

Mornings feature our Conspiracy Coffee Bar serving coffee and tea to ease into the day. As the sun sets, the space transforms into an open-air cinema with Lebowski-inspired films, paired with snacks and White Russians.

Throughout the day, enjoy a variety of interactive games including Disc-O-Golf, bocce, Magic Stones, Truth-or-Dare Jenga, and classic dice and card games. Relax in our hammock-filled lounge and stay awhile.

Abide, play, and connect.
column10:
Abiding Grove is a Dudeist-inspired theme camp focused on a relaxed, interactive lounge environment. We offer a coffee/tea gathering space in the mornings, casual games throughout the day, and movie viewing in the evenings. The camp emphasizes low-pressure social interaction, rest, and playful engagement.

Total Space Requested

130 ft x 130 ft (includes public space, camp infrastructure, and camper living area)

Core Layout Elements

Central shaded lounge with hammocks, chairs, and seating
Coffee/tea bar (“Conspiracy Coffee Bar”) for morning service
Evening movie area with screen and seating
Distributed game zones to encourage flow and reduce crowding
10 ft x 10 ft reserved space for potential shuttle stop

Key Activity Space Requirements

Bocce / Bowling Court: 15 ft x 90 ft
Requires long, flat, level ground (critical)
Disc-O-Golf Area: 30 ft x 30 ft
Outdoor Dance Pole Area: 25 ft x 25 ft

Additional Activities

Bocce ball
Magic Stones
Truth-or-Dare Jenga
Dice and card games
Casual lounge-based play areas

Infrastructure & Operations

Shade structures over lounge and bar
Ambient lighting for safety and atmosphere (not a sound/light-heavy camp)
Moderate sound levels
Light-duty bar/kitchen (coffee, tea, simple cocktails, snacks only)

Daily Flow

Morning: Coffee and tea service
Daytime: Open lounge and games
Evening: Movie viewing with snacks and cocktails
After movie: Transitions to open lounge only (no active service)

Placement Preferences

Near portopotties strongly preferred (due to coffee/tea service and older camp members)
Flat terrain required, especially for 90 ft bocce/bowling lane
Previously placed at corner of Lunar Loop & Callisto Way (across from Sanctuary) — this location worked very well (flat, visible, good participant flow). Similar conditions preferred.

Camp Environment / Sound

Not a high-volume or sound-focused camp
Social, conversational, and relaxed atmosphere
Designed as a recharge space rather than a party zone

column9:
Hello there! Kamp Khaos is here! We have landed upon this realm to bring you a sight beyond sanity! A fun filled family friendly place to drag your siblings, friends, or whomever and beat them in the face! We are a fully immersive combat-oriented gladiator event just itching for you to take the first step unto GLORY!!
    Do you have what it takes to claw your way to the top? Can you, the wandering hipster, crush the competition and proclaim yourself the absolute winner!?! Do you have it in you to take on grandma and her flying dentures of doom? Can you survive the humiliation of that walker grinding your bones to dust as you lie defeated and broken on the floor? WHO KNOWS?! Come let us find out in the realms of Kamp Khaos where everyone wins!
column10:
Kamp Khaos is an interactive game that all can enjoy. Participants who choose to fully immerse themselves receive a fitted helmet, armor, and their choice of weapon to use for the duration. This comes with the full understanding that one is to unleash the havoc within upon a fully suspecting and possibly terrified (willing) opponent. Those who choose to vicariously participate can enjoy playing the part of the bloodthirsty MOB!! The MOB is a crucial component to the show. Just like in the Roman gladiator games of old we still need judgement from YOU!! Many times, there have been no clear victor and when such a thing happens The Ringmaster, Khaos, will reach out to the MOB for the final say. The roars of the crowd determine the fate of all. Which player achieved glorious victory, and which player is cast down in defeat only to rise again? You decide! 
   The rules are fairly simple and must be followed at all times. There are eight in total, but in short, these three are the basics:
1 - Two gladiators battle the best 2 out of 3 rounds to win the game.
2 - Push your opponent out of the inner marked ring of the Arena to win. If you or your opponent is unable to do so then you have one minute per round to beat the ever lovin shnikeys out of each other with the final ruling of a draw going to the Mob. 
3 - Finally, all participants will follow the Ringmaster’s directions at all times or be bounced from the game permanently.

column10:
“Creature Comforts” is a small but mighty camp of 10-15 mostly experienced burners devoted to the subtle luxuries that make a big difference. Our pop-up “shop front” offers essential comfort items that participants may have forgotten in their otherworldly travels to the woods or just across the grounds; hand warmers for cooler nights, hand sanitizer for post-porta visits, feminine hygiene products, ear plugs for much-needed rest, band aides for that pesky blister, and other small but meaningful supports - as well as a MOOP station for discarded packaging!

With a restock each morning, Creature Comforts is rooted in gifting and communal care 24/7 during our stay. We provide simple tools that help fellow burners stay grounded, comfortable, and ready to fully participate in the magic of the week.

column9:
Sacred Swarm is an immersive hive-inspired playground centered around a glowing 32-foot geodesic dome transformed into a surreal adult jungle gym for the senses. Inspired by the communal intelligence and matriarchal structure of a beehive, the dome becomes a living swarm of movement, spectacle, and collective energy, as “worker bees” swing, lounge, and buzz through this space designed for exploration, connection, play, and wonder.

Complementing the playground is a communal altar honoring personal icons, ancestors, artists, and visionaries who inspire us. Participants are invited to contribute offerings and symbols celebrating the divinity within ourselves and the people who shape our lives.

Part sacred sanctuary, part cosmic circus, Sacred Swarm invites you to rest, play, connect, and lose yourself in the hum of the hive.

(Brought to you by Camp MEOWee WOWee)

column28: We minimize what we bring, prioritizing reusable materials and bulk supplies. Our camp does daily MOOP sweeps with everyone responsible for picking up, and our bar operates with reusable cups only. All gray water is contained and properly disposed of. During teardown, we conduct line sweeps until the site is spotless. Every campmate is accountable so we leave our space better than we found it.
column29: We’ll keep everyone aware of volunteer opportunities through a designated camp point person who shares available shifts before and during the burn. Updates will go out in our group chat so they’re easy to access in real time. We’ll encourage everyone to take at least one shift if they’re able, and make it simple to find and sign up for roles that fit their vibe and energy.

column9: Get Attune-UP in our interactive empathy practice installation and draw or write on the Heart Car.
column10: Sorry for late submission and I understand if we can’t be included as a camp at this late hour. I had been meaning to take a break from leading a theme camp but would still like to be able to post events on the dust app. Heart Mechanics is a two person camp from St. Louis bringing an interactive empathy practice installation that is already on the placement map and is called Attune-UP. It’s a 13x26 pop up canopy housing five art chairs that may be sat in as functional seats and/or used to guide participants through five different ways we can relate to our experience at any given moment. We are also bringing a car that will be parked the duration of the burn called The Heart Car and it has sharpies for participants to draw or write on throughout the burn. We usually offer daily silent meditations as well as at least one demo of how to use the chairs and may add a kids session to read story books and do arts and crafts related to emotional intelligence. Visit heart-mechanics.com to learn more about us.
